Colchicine Pilot Trial — Statistical Results

July 23, 2026

Colchicine Pilot Trial — Statistical Results

Protocol: STUDY-21-001175, version 3.0 (22 May 2023) | Trial registration: NCT05279690 | Data cut: 23 July 2026

Primary Assessment Method

Table 1. Primary assessment method.
Primary assessment Value
Title Maximum percent decline in CRP from baseline (cycle 1 day 1) during post-baseline C1 on-treatment visits
Number of patients who signed informed consent 16
Number of patients enrolled 11
Number of screen failures before enrollment 5
Number evaluable for toxicity 11
Number evaluable for primary efficacy (CRP) 11
Planned evaluable for primary efficacy (protocol) 15
Endpoint definition Peripheral blood CRP (mg/L) at cycle 1 visits; baseline = cycle 1 day 1 (or screening if missing); minimum post-baseline value at cycle 1 day 8 or day 15 used for maximum decline. Cycle 2 values excluded per protocol treatment window.
Null hypothesis (H0) Mean maximum percent CRP decline = 0 (colchicine does not reduce post-treatment CRP)
Alternative hypothesis (H1, one-sided) Mean maximum percent CRP decline > 0
Statistical test One-sample one-sided t-test on patient-level maximum percent CRP declines
Significance level (α) α = 0.025 per protocol Section 12.4 (Bonferroni adjustment of overall α = 0.05 across two planned Cohort 1 primary tests: low-dose vs 0 and high-dose vs low-dose; high-dose arm did not enroll, so only the low-dose one-sample test was performed)
Post-hoc statistical power (actual N, α = 0.025) Approximately 50% to detect the observed effect (standardized effect size d ≈ 0.65) with 11 evaluable patients (protocol planned 15; design target 80% power at d = 0.80)
Primary analysis result (Cohort 1 low dose) Mean max decline 18.7% (SD 28.5%); one-sided one-sample t-test vs 0, t = 2.17, p = 0.028; does not reject H0 at prespecified α = 0.025

Accrual Disposition

All 16 subjects who signed informed consent underwent screening evaluation. Protocol v3.0 specified two Cohort 1 treatment schedules: the original schedule (indefinite on-study colchicine with serial imaging surveillance) and a fixed 2-week course without transitioning patients already on the original schedule. 7 enrolled patients are included in Kaplan–Meier progression-free and overall survival analyses; 4 enrolled on the fixed 2-week schedule contributed to toxicity and primary CRP analyses but were excluded from survival estimates because they did not follow the same longitudinal imaging schedule. When organ-function or CRP thresholds were not met, the report states the clinical reason for screen failure and the screening laboratory value when available.

Fixed 2-week schedule (phase 0 intervention) 4 4 (primary CRP / toxicity only)
Screen failure before enrollment 5 5 (31.2% of consented)
Original schedule 3 3 (Kaplan–Meier–evaluable)
Fixed 2-week schedule 2 2 (primary CRP / toxicity only)

Primary Assessment Results

The primary endpoint was tested per protocol Section 12.4 at prespecified H0: mean maximum CRP decline = 0 versus H1: mean decline > 0 (one-sided), with α = 0.025 after Bonferroni adjustment for two planned Cohort 1 tests; only the low-dose one-sample test was performed because the high-dose arm did not enroll. 11 of 15 planned evaluable patients were analyzed; post-hoc power to detect the observed effect at α = 0.025 was approximately 50% (protocol design target: 80% power at 15 evaluable patients).

Figure 1. Maximum percent decline in C-reactive protein (CRP) from baseline during on-treatment assessment by enrolled patient (Cohort 1 low dose). Abbreviations: CRP, C-reactive protein.

Figure 2. Best percent change in target-lesion sum of diameters during continued colchicine treatment. The waterfall plot shows the best percent change from baseline among five patients treated on the original schedule; the accompanying table summarizes scan timing, CRP change near the best-response scan, and prior immunotherapy exposure. One patient, ID 4, experienced tumor shrinkage of 19% after prior ipilimumab plus nivolumab, although CRP increased near the time of the scan. This post hoc analysis is hypothesis-generating only. Abbreviations: CRP, C-reactive protein; IO, immunotherapy; SOD, sum of diameters.

Figure 3. Longitudinal peripheral blood CRP (mg/L) across protocol C1 visits (baseline C1D1 and post-baseline C1D8/C1D15) for enrolled patients with serial C1 values. Abbreviations: CRP, C-reactive protein.

General Toxicity Profile

Treatment-related adverse events (TRAE; attribution coded as Possible/Probable/Definite) are summarized by worst CTCAE grade per patient per preferred term (if multiple grades are recorded for the same term, only the maximum grade is counted).

Nausea Grade 1 1 (9.1%)
Vomiting Grade 1 1 (9.1%)
Weight loss Grade 1 1 (9.1%)

Pharmacokinetics and Pharmacodynamics

Pharmacokinetics were not evaluated. Pharmacodynamic assessments included CRP (primary), ferritin, and erythrocyte sedimentation rate (ESR) when collected per protocol.

Patient Status and Survival

Progression-free survival (PFS) and overall survival (OS) were not recorded on the dedicated case report forms in the available dataset. Endpoints below were derived per patient from: (1) RECIST 1.1 best overall response (progressive disease [PD] date); (2) protocol therapy discontinuation for documented progression; (3) final subject status and date of death or last contact. Time zero = first investigational product dispense date, or enrollment if missing.

Analyses in this section include only the 7 patients enrolled under the original indefinite colchicine schedule with serial imaging surveillance. The 4 patients enrolled on the fixed 2-week schedule lacked the same longitudinal imaging framework and are reported for CRP pharmacodynamics and safety only.

Table 11. Exploratory best overall response (RECIST 1.1) among original-schedule enrolled patients (Kaplan–Meier cohort).

Figure 4. Progression-free survival (Kaplan–Meier) from treatment start among patients enrolled under the original indefinite colchicine schedule with surveillance imaging (n = 7; exploratory; not powered). PFS events: RECIST PD or discontinuation for progression. Abbreviations: PFS, progression-free survival; PD, progressive disease; RECIST, Response Evaluation Criteria in Solid Tumors.

Figure 5. Overall survival (Kaplan–Meier) from treatment start among patients enrolled under the original indefinite colchicine schedule with surveillance imaging (n = 7; exploratory; not powered). Abbreviations: OS, overall survival.

These survival analyses are descriptive and underpowered (Kaplan–Meier cohort n = 7; total enrolled n = 11). Medians and curves should be interpreted cautiously; several patients lack formal final-status forms but have tumor assessment or visit dates used for censoring. Patients on the fixed 2-week schedule (n = 4) are excluded because they did not receive the same longitudinal imaging surveillance framework.

Statistical Analysis

Primary hypothesis and test

Primary analysis (Cohort 1 low dose). The primary endpoint was maximum percent decline in peripheral blood CRP from cycle 1 day 1 to the lowest on-treatment value during post-baseline cycle 1 visits (cycle 1 day 8 or day 15). We tested the prespecified null hypothesis H0: mean maximum CRP decline = 0 against the one-sided alternative H1: mean decline > 0 using a one-sample t-test. Per protocol STUDY-21-001175, version 3.0 (22 May 2023) Section 12.4, the overall Cohort 1 type I error was controlled at 0.05 across two planned primary tests (low-dose vs 0 and high-dose vs low-dose) using Bonferroni’s method, yielding α = 0.025 for each test. Because the high-dose sequential arm did not enroll, only the low-dose one-sample test was performed at α = 0.025. The observed mean decline was 18.7% (SD 28.5%); one-sided p = 0.028, which does not reject H0 at the prespecified α = 0.025 (the primary endpoint was not met because p > 0.025, not because p > 0.05).

Sample size and power. The protocol planned 15 evaluable patients in the low-dose cohort (80% power to detect a standardized effect size of 0.80 at α = 0.025). 11 evaluable patients were analyzed. Post-hoc power to detect the observed effect (standardized effect size d ≈ 0.65) at α = 0.025 was approximately 50%. At the full planned sample size of 15 with the same observed effect, post-hoc power would have been approximately 65% — still below the protocol design target because the observed effect was smaller than the protocol assumption (d = 0.80).

Note: Only one of two Bonferroni-planned primary comparisons was conducted because the high-dose arm never opened. The prespecified significance threshold for this report remains α = 0.025; a nominal α = 0.05 threshold is not applied post hoc.

Exploratory and safety analyses

Exploratory progression-free and overall survival estimates used the Kaplan–Meier method among the 7 patients enrolled under the original indefinite-treatment schedule with serial imaging surveillance only. PFS and OS were derived from RECIST 1.1 assessments, protocol therapy discontinuation for progression, and final subject status when dedicated progression forms were incomplete. Treatment-related adverse events required Possible, Probable, or Definite attribution to colchicine and were summarized by CTCAE v5.0 preferred term and worst grade per patient. Analyses followed protocol STUDY-21-001175, version 3.0 (22 May 2023) Section 12. Supplementary statistical methods and analysis output are available separately.
